research_interests.research_description_pi_profile: My long term goals as a clinician scientist are 1) to advance our understanding of vitamin D metabolism and of the role of vitamin D in physiology and 2) to apply mechanistic insights gained from this understanding to develop and modify rational, targeted and effective therapeutic interventions to improve the health of patients with endocrine disease.
research_interests.research_description_pi_profile_cont: Our labs current efforts have focused on the role of CYP2R1 in vitamin D metabolism and physiology. This work leverages observations from genomic studies to try to understand the biochemistry of 25-hydroxylation of vitamin D.
research_interests.note: These descriptions are from ~2015-2016 institutional forms. The lab's scope has since expanded significantly to include sarcopenia, PCOS, immune modulation, CAR-Treg approaches for T1DM, and tumor biology. See CLAUDE.md for current research narrative.

background_statement: Dr. Roizen is a clinician scientist at the Children's Hospital of Philadelphia. His clinical interests include metabolic bone diseases with a focus on vitamin D metabolism, hyperparathyroidism and pseudohypoparathyroidism as well as obesity, type 2 diabetes and type 1 diabetes. Dr. Roizen has more than 30 papers, patents and chapters.

website_usable_content.hero_bio: Dr. Jeffrey Roizen is a physician-scientist at the Children's Hospital of Philadelphia and the University of Pennsylvania, where he holds a tenure-track appointment as Assistant Professor in the Division of Endocrinology and Diabetes.
website_usable_content.credentials_line: MD, PhD (Washington University in St. Louis) | Board Certified in Pediatrics and Pediatric Endocrinology | Fellow of the American Academy of Pediatrics
website_usable_content.training_narrative: Jeff trained at Washington University in St. Louis, earning both his MD and PhD, before completing his residency and fellowship at CHOP — where he has remained ever since, building an independent research program at the intersection of vitamin D biology and clinical endocrinology.
website_usable_content.clinical_narrative: As a practicing pediatric endocrinologist, Dr. Roizen cares for children with metabolic bone diseases, disorders of calcium metabolism, diabetes, and obesity. This clinical work directly informs the lab's research questions — every project connects back to a patient population.
website_usable_content.awards_narrative: Jeff has received early-career recognition from nearly every major society in his field, including the Endocrine Society's AMGEN Scholars Award (2013) and Early Investigator Award (2016), the Pediatric Endocrine Society's Clinical Scholar Award (2015), and the ASBMR Presidential Poster Award (2012).
website_usable_content.mentors_narrative: Jeff's scientific training was shaped by Dr. Michael Levine and Dr. Hakon Hakonarson at CHOP, whose complementary expertise in mineral metabolism and genomics helped establish the foundation for the Roizen Lab's integrative approach to vitamin D biology.
